response:
### Part 1: General Formula for Pages Read Each Day

We are given the number of pages read on three consecutive days:

- Day 1: 5 pages  
- Day 2: 12 pages  
- Day 3: 21 pages  

This forms a quadratic sequence, meaning the number of pages read on day $ n $ is given by a quadratic function:

$$
P(n) = an^2 + bn + c
$$

We can use the three given values to set up a system of equations:

1. $ P(1) = a(1)^2 + b(1) + c = a + b + c = 5 $  
2. $ P(2) = a(2)^2 + b(2) + c = 4a + 2b + c = 12 $  
3. $ P(3) = a(3)^2 + b(3) + c = 9a + 3b + c = 21 $

Now solve the system:

From equation 1:  
$ a + b + c = 5 $ --- (i)

From equation 2:  
$ 4a + 2b + c = 12 $ --- (ii)

From equation 3:  
$ 9a + 3b + c = 21 $ --- (iii)

Subtract equation (i) from equation (ii):

$$
(4a + 2b + c) - (a + b + c) = 12 - 5 \Rightarrow 3a + b = 7 \quad \text{(iv)}
$$

Subtract equation (ii) from equation (iii):

$$
(9a + 3b + c) - (4a + 2b + c) = 21 - 12 \Rightarrow 5a + b = 9 \quad \text{(v)}
$$

Now subtract equation (iv) from equation (v):

$$
(5a + b) - (3a + b) = 9 - 7 \Rightarrow 2a = 2 \Rightarrow a = 1
$$

Substitute $ a = 1 $ into equation (iv):

$$
3(1) + b = 7 \Rightarrow b = 4
$$

$$
1 +
